name: Tyrfing Full
description: Tyrfing, also known as Tyrfingr, is a cursed sword from Norse mythology, appearing in the Hervarar saga and other Old Norse texts. Forged by the dwarves Dvalinn and Durin for King Svafrlami, it was imbued with a potent curse: it would never miss a strike, it would never be sheathed without blood on it, and it would ultimately cause the death of its owner. Svafrlami trapped the dwarves and forced them to forge the blade, but they exacted their revenge by cursing it. The sword passed through many hands, bringing misfortune and death to all who possessed it. Notable owners include Arngrim and Angantyr. Tyrfing's insatiable thirst for battle and its inherent curse made it a fearsome and dangerous weapon, a symbol of destructive power and the inescapable nature of fate. Its gleam was said to be brighter than fire, and its edge sharper than any other blade. It played a pivotal role in several key battles and ultimately led to the downfall of entire bloodlines, solidifying its reputation as one of the most infamous and tragic artifacts in Norse lore. The sword's legacy serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of unchecked power and the consequences of greed and violence. Its existence challenges the notions of heroism and honor, highlighting the dark side of human ambition and the enduring power of curses.
subtitle: The Cursed Sword of Unending Battle
origin: Forged by the dwarves Dvalinn and Durin under duress for King Svafrlami.
currentLocation: Lost to time, possibly buried with Angantyr on Samsø.

keyMyths.title: The Forging of Tyrfing
keyMyths.description: King Svafrlami captures the dwarves Dvalinn and Durin, forcing them to forge a powerful sword. The dwarves curse the sword, ensuring it will cause death and misfortune.

keyMyths.title: Tyrfing's Curse Fulfilled
keyMyths.description: The sword passes through generations, leaving a trail of death and destruction in its wake. Numerous battles are fought, and many lives are lost due to Tyrfing's insatiable bloodlust.

sources.author: Unknown
